body.title: Wire consult hypotheses back into SITUATE as file-path hints (consult v2)
body.result: Consult→SITUATE feedback loop closed. ticket_description in consult extra; _files_from_consult_hints extracts paths from hypotheses; tier.2-unavailable now also tries consult. 3984 tests pass. b61f090c
body.description:
consult_results land in basket['consult_results'] but are never read by pe_situate (detection-only v1 per code comment). This means SITUATE fires a consult when it returns 0 files, gets hypotheses back, and then returns empty anyway — the hypotheses have zero effect on the chain. Two gaps to close:

1. The consult state at situate_empty doesn't include the ticket description (basket['description']), so the peer LLM receives only 'SITUATE returned 0 files' with no context about what the ticket is trying to do. Hypotheses come back vague and can't name files.

2. Even if hypotheses were file-specific, pe_situate returns empty without reading basket['consult_results']. The feedback loop doesn't exist.

Fix: (a) Pass basket['description'] in extra when building ConsultState at the situate_empty call site. (b) After _maybe_consult_stuck fires at situate_empty, read the returned hypotheses from basket['consult_results'], extract path-like strings (regex for .py / subdir patterns) OR pass the hypothesis text as a hint to a second tier.2 SITUATE call. If that second call returns files, use them. If still empty, escalate as before.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/tools/pe_chain.py (pe_situate + the situate_empty call site in _maybe_consult_stuck)
**Design rules:** docs-in-code applies (pe_chain.py has architectural doc block — update Updated timestamp). No new memory schemas. Test plan below.
**Scope boundary:** IN: pe_situate feedback loop for situate_empty only. OUT: Other consult hook sites (preflight, implement-fail) — separate tickets if needed.
**Test plan:** Add test: mock _maybe_consult_stuck to inject a consult_result with file-path hypothesis; assert pe_situate returns those files rather than empty. Existing SITUATE tests must still pass. Run: python -m pytest tests/ -x -q -k 'situate'
